subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the basic terms related to a circle: radius, diameter, chord, circumference
Learn the properties of tangents and secants
Apply simple theorems related to circles
Identify parts of a circle in diagrams
Solve straightforward numerical problems involving circle properties

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Circle
topics_covered: Parts of a circle: radius, diameter, chord, circumference
student_activities: Draw a circle and label radius, diameter, and chord
Identify these parts in given diagrams

lesson_plan.title: Angle Properties of Circles
topics_covered: Angle subtended by a chord at the center
Angle subtended by the same chord on the circle
Relation between angles at center and circumference
student_activities: Draw chords and angles in circle diagrams

lesson_plan.title: Tangent to a Circle
topics_covered: Properties: tangent perpendicular to radius at point of contact
student_activities: Draw tangent to a given circle

lesson_plan.title: Secant and Chord Properties
topics_covered: Equal chords subtend equal angles at the center

lesson_plan.title: Simple Numerical Problems
topics_covered: Finding lengths of chords and segments
Use of radius and diameter in problems
teaching_strategy: Encourage students to calculate with formulas

lesson_plan.title: Chapter Revision and Class Quiz
topics_covered: Properties of chords, tangents, and secants
teaching_strategy: Quick recap of all formulas and theorems

teacher_tip: Emphasize understanding the definitions and basic properties first; practical drawing exercises make concepts of chords and tangents easier to grasp.
